Artifacts.Name: Module Topics
Artifacts.Description: A structured outline of all topics covered in the module, spanning function definitions, parameters, *args/**kwargs, scope rules, comprehensions, and functional patterns, used by learners and instructors to navigate the module's learning objectives.

Artifacts.Name: Module Topics (HTML)
Artifacts.Description: This artifact is a structured outline listing the topics covered in Module 3, including defining functions, comprehensions, and functional patterns in Python, and serves as a navigational overview for learners and instructors to see what concepts will be taught and in what order.

Artifacts.Name: Future Value: While Loop Input and Present Value Calculation Assignment
Artifacts.Description: An assignment page instructing students to write a Python program using a while loop to calculate the Present Value of equipment resale amounts and annual revenues across multiple cost scenarios at a 5% interest rate, used by learners to practice loops and engineering economics calculations.

Artifacts.Name: Functions_Comprehensions_and
Artifacts.Description: An interactive reading page introducing Python function definitions using the def and return statements along with docstrings, used by learners to understand how to write, call, and document reusable functions.

Artifacts.Name: Functions_Comprehensions_and
Artifacts.Description: An interactive reading page covering Python's LEGB scope rule and the global and nonlocal keywords, used by learners to understand how variable names are resolved across local, enclosing, global, and built-in scopes.

Artifacts.Name: Functions_Comprehensions_and
Artifacts.Description: An interactive reading page covering Python's LEGB scope rule and the global and nonlocal keywords, used by learners to understand how variable names are resolved across local, enclosing, global, and built-in scopes.

Artifacts.Name: Using a While Loop for Input and Calculating Present Value
Artifacts.Description: An assignment page directing students to write a Python while-loop program that evaluates multiple equipment purchase scenarios by calculating Present Value of resale and revenue streams over a 10-year lifespan at 5% interest, used by learners to apply engineering economics concepts in code.

Artifacts.Name: Base Case or Recursive Case?
Artifacts.Description: A ten-round interactive quiz presenting hidden blocks from Python recursive functions and asking learners to classify each block as either a base case or a recursive case, used to reinforce understanding of recursion structure.

Artifacts.Name: External Materials Index
Artifacts.Description: An index page listing one external assignment resource—the Present Value while-loop engineering economics assignment—used by instructors and learners to access supplementary materials linked to the module.
